ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE, INC.'S GENERAL DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AND ITS SPECIAL DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LOSS OF CONSORTIUM IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE GENERAL DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO GIVE PLAINTIFFS AN OPPORTUNITY, IF THEY ARE ABLE TO DO SO, TO ALLEGE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF BOCKRATH AND OF C.C.P. 340.2 (STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS). THE SPECIAL DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AN ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT FOR BOTH THE DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FURTHER DETAILS OF THE RULING.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 503, DEFENDANTS FOSTER WHEELER ENERGY CORPORATION, UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION, PNEUMO ABEX LLC'S MOTION TO QUASH PLAINTIFFS NOTICE OF TAKING VIDEOTAPED DEPOSITION OF WILLIAM MCRAY, SR., AND FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANTS FOSTER WHEELER ENERGY CORPORATION, PNEUMO ABEX LLC AND UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO QUASH PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF TAKING VIDEOTAPED DEPOSITION OF WILLIAM MCRAY, SR. IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. AS TO THE MOTION TO QUASH, DEFENDANTS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THEY SERVED WRITTEN OBJECTIONS TO THE DEPOSITION NOTICE SO THEY WAIVED ANY ERROR OR IRREGULARITY IN THE NOTICE. (C.C.P. 2025.410(A).) AS TO THE MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER, DEFENDANTS' MOTION IS GRANTED IN THAT THE DEPOSITION MUST NOT OCCUR ON SATURDAYS, SUNDAYS OR HOLIDAYS. THE MOTION IS DENIED IN THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT HAVE TO PROVIDE DEFENDANTS WITH THE REQUESTED INFORMATION (SHIP LISTS, AUTO LISTS, ETC.) BECAUSE DEFENDANTS CITED NO AUTHORITY THAT REQUIRES SUCH INFORMATION BE PRODUCED. THE MOTION IS FURTHER DENIED IN THAT THE DEPOSITION WILL TAKE PLACE AT THE LOCATION SPECIFIED IN PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED NOTICE. IT WILL NOT TAKE PLACE IN THE BAY AREA. (SEE C.C.P. 2025.250(A).) THE STAY OF THE DEPOSITION IS LIFTED. DEFENDANTS' AND PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 503, DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE, INC.'S JOINDER IN DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO QUASH PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF TAKING VIDEOTAPED DEPOSITION OF WILLIAM MCRAY, SR. NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DENIED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. AS TO THE MOTION TO QUASH, DEFENDANTS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THEY SERVED WRITTEN OBJECTIONS TO THE DEPOSITION NOTICE SO THEY WAIVED ANY ERROR OR IRREGULARITY IN THE NOTICE. (C.C.P. 2025.410(A).) AS TO THE MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER, DEFENDANTS' MOTION IS GRANTED IN THAT THE DEPOSITION MUST NOT OCCUR ON SATURDAYS, SUNDAYS OR HOLIDAYS. THE MOTION IS DENIED IN THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT HAVE TO PROVIDE DEFENDANTS WITH THE REQUESTED INFORMATION (SHIP LISTS, AUTO LISTS, ETC.) BECAUSE DEFENDANTS CITED NO AUTHORITY THAT REQUIRES SUCH INFORMATION BE PRODUCED. THE MOTION IS FURTHER DENIED IN THAT THE DEPOSITION WILL TAKE PLACE AT THE LOCATION SPECIFIED IN PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED NOTICE. IT WILL NOT TAKE PLACE IN THE BAY AREA. (SEE C.C.P. 2025.250(A).) THE STAY OF THE DEPOSITION IS LIFTED. DEFENDANTS' AND PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Notice Of Taking Videotaped Deposition Of William Mcray, Sr., And For Protective Order
Off Calendar Dec-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Shelley K. Tinkoff, Esq., of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendants / moving parties Foster Wheeler Energy Corporation, Pneumo Abex LLC, sucessor in interest to Abex Corporation and Union Carbide Corporation
Kelly McMeekin, Esq., of NAPOLI, BERN, RIPKA & SHKOLNIK, LLP (310-536-1040), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties William McRay, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANTS FOSTER WHEELER ENERGY CORPORATION, UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION, PNEUMO ABEX LLC'S MOTION TO QUASH PLAINTIFFS NOTICE OF TAKING VIDEOTAPED DEPOSITION OF WILLIAM MCRAY, SR., AND FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendants Foster Wheeler Energy Corporation, Pneumo Abex LLC and Union Carbide Corporation's motion to quash plaintiff's notice of taking videotaped deposition of William McRay, Sr. is denied. Defendant's motion for protective order is granted in part and denied in part. As to the motion to quash, defendants failed to demonstrate that they served written objections to the deposition notice so they waived any error or irregularity in the notice. (C.C.P. 2025.410(a).) As to the motion for protective order, defendants' motion is granted in that the deposition must not occur on Saturdays, Sundays or holidays. The motion is denied in that plaintiff does not have to provide defendants with the requested information (ship lists, auto lists, etc.) because defendants cited no authority that requires such information be produced. The motion is further denied in that the deposition will take place at the location specified in plaintiff's amended notice. It will not take place in the Bay Area. (See C.C.P. 2025.250(a).) The stay of the deposition is lifted. Defendants' and plaintiff's requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)

MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant John Crane, Inc.'s joinder in defendants' motion to quash plaintiff's notice of taking videotaped deposition of William McRay, Sr. is denied. Defendant's motion for protective order is granted in part and denied in part. As to the motion to quash, defendants failed to demonstrate that they served written objections to the deposition notice so they waived any error or irregularity in the notice. (C.C.P. 2025.410(a).) As to the motion for protective order, defendants' motion is granted in that the deposition must not occur on Saturdays, Sundays or holidays. The motion is denied in that plaintiff does not have to provide defendants with the requested information (ship lists, auto lists, etc.) because defendants cited no authority that requires such information be produced. The motion is further denied in that the deposition will take place at the location specified in plaintiff's amended notice. It will not take place in the Bay Area. (See C.C.P. 2025.250(a).) The stay of the deposition is lifted. Defendants' and plaintiff's requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Brendan J. Tuohy, Esq., of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8200), appearing for defendant / moving party Crane Co.
Kurt Putnam, Esq., of HASSARD BONNINGTON (415-288-9800), appearing for defendant / moving party John Crane, Inc.
Marc I. Willick, Esq., of NAPOLI, BERN, RIPKA & ASSOCIATES, LLP (212-267-3700), appearing via CourtCall for plaintiffs / opposing parties William M. McCray, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s demurrer to the sixth cause of action for loss of consortium is sustained without leave to amend. Since all of Mr. McRay's claimed exposure to asbestos pre-dated his marriage with Mrs. McRay, Mrs. McRay does not have a viable claim for loss of consortium. Plaintiffs' argument that Zwicker is inapplicable is belied by the language of Zwicker. (See also Owens-Illinois, Inc. v. Cook (MD 2005) 872 A.2d 969, 982-84 (an asbestos exposure case which refers to Zwicker as among those cases that hold that "the marriage requirement for a loss of consortium claim is absolute, [and] that if the marriage post-dated the injury, there is no consortium claim.")). An order was signed in open court for both the demurrer and motion to strike. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Brendan J. Tuohy, Esq., of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8200), appearing for defendant / moving party Crane Co.
Kurt Putnam, Esq., of HASSARD BONNINGTON (415-288-9800), appearing for defendant / moving party John Crane, Inc.
Marc I. Willick, Esq., of NAPOLI, BERN, RIPKA & ASSOCIATES, LLP (212-267-3700), appearing via CourtCall for plaintiffs / opposing parties William M. McCray, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant John Crane, Inc.'s general demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is sustained with leave to amend and its special demurrer to the sixth cause of action for loss of consortium is sustained without leave to amend. The general demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath and of C.C.P. 340.2 (statute of limitations). The special demurrer to the sixth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Since all of Mr. McRay's claimed exposure to asbestos pre-dated his marriage with Mrs. McRay, Mrs. McRay does not have a viable claim for loss of consortium. Plaintiffs' argument that Zwicker is inapplicable is belied by the language of Zwicker. (See also Owens-Illinois, Inc. v. Cook (MD 2005) 872 A.2d 969, 982-84 (an asbestos exposure case which refers to Zwicker as among those cases that hold that "the marriage requirement for a loss of consortium claim is absolute, [and] that if the marriage post-dated the injury, there is no consortium claim.")). If plaintiffs choose to file an amended complaint, the court encourages plaintiffs to address defendant's arguments regarding the application of CCP 340.2, but the court is not now making any ruling on that issue. An order was signed in open court for both the demurrer and motion to strike. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
